Okay, division of roles. So this is for those of you who run your business with a spouse or partner. Okay. So this is the way that we see it and this is why this is so important. Because a lot of you that do run this with your spouse ask Alex and I quite often, you know, what has made you successful? How do you guys do it? All these things. And so I want to share with you. It's very simple. It's just that most people don't do the simple things because they sound too easy to be true. Right. So one, just giving you some perspective, you have your relationship with yourself. And that's your first relationship, right? And then you have your relationship with your spouse. And that's your second relationship. And so these two combined are going to shape your entire world, as well as the lens that you see from. Okay. That is why we talk about our partnership as the foundation upon which we build our lives on. So I believe that your partnership creates the success or failure you have spiritually, the success or failure you have financially, the success or failure you have with your relationships, the success or failure you have in your business, the success or failure you have with your health and wellness. Okay. Because whether we like it or not, you know, everyone talks about the five people you spend the most time with. Well, think about the one person you spend the most time with and that you build your life with. Okay. It's incredibly important that you are aligned. Therefore, choosing the right partner and working your ass off can either set you up for success, for failure or somewhere in between. Okay. And so if you choose to work together, you must have the following elements to allow a successful relationship to flourish. Okay. It's just, it's, you must. So here's the things that if you're working together, you need to have in place. One is your shared mission. Two is your aligned values. Three clear expectations. Four is trust. And five is transparency. Shared mission. This should be your client-centric mission for the most part. Okay. Because if you are a purpose, if you are a mission-driven business, then you both should have the same mission in mind. Okay. And you both need to be on the same page with that. Right. Second is your aligned values. Alex and I like to keep it as simple as possible. And so our personal values are also the values in business. I don't think there is a difference. If for some reason you think differently, I guess you could make core values with your spouse. However, I suggest that you just make the core values you have be applicable to your business and your relationships. Right. Clear expectations. So that would be division of roles, which we're going to discuss next. Trust, which is that once you delegate or divide roles, you must trust your spouse entirely. Okay. And so just like you need to trust your teammates and you must extend the trust before you have seen the results, you must do so with your spouse. This applies not only to your business, but to your personal life. Okay. And then lastly is transparency. Remembering that there's nobody else that you share the human experience with aside from your partner or spouse. Okay. And so if you can't be real with them about the business, about how you feel, about the things that are bothering you, about what's taking your attention, then who can you be with? There's truly nobody else. So you must understand your relationship is either going to set you up for success or failure. Okay. And I tell you that from personal experience. So what does this look like? I made this for one of the legacy presentations and I think it's just a good visual representation. Right. Your shared mission is the absolute brick foundation. If you don't have the cement poured, then everything else can crumble one day. Right. Your aligned values. That's what's reinforcing it. You know, what values do you use to run your lives? Clear expectations because you can have aligned values and mission, but if you don't have clear expectations, I like to think that unspoken expectations are the death of relationships. And so you have to be clear about what you do, what I do, what I want from you, what you want from me. Right. And then trust, which is the pillars that you build the roof and the windows with and the transparency so that you can see in and you can have perspective. Right. Makes it very simple if you build your relationship with these concepts in mind.
